items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

articles.title: Victoria ended native forest logging. So where are these trees coming from?

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

image.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

items.title: Hobart's Southern Outlet widening project hit by $32m blowout

children.content: The cost of widening Hobart's Southern Outlet has blown out by another $32 million, with completion of the northern section of the project now estimated at $95 million.

image.altText: Southern Outlet Hobart

items.title: Potential students allege visa scam tied to education consultancy

children.content: Aspiring students looking to study abroad in Australia say they've been left blindsided and with their futures in doubt after an education consultancy promised them migration and visa advice.

image.altText: A graphic pictures a thoughtful woman and text reading 'Visa expiring soon? Discuss your options'.

items.title: Marinus will break energy 'deadlock' hobbling Tasmania, economist says

children.content: An economist says Marinus Link remains an investment in the national interest and will shore up Tasmania's energy security. It follows a report last week questioning the project's business case.

image.altText: An artists rendition of a large cable coming out of a blue ocean.

items.title: Nude swimmers take chilly plunge in Dark Mofo solstice swim

children.content: Dark Mofo's Nude Solstice Swim marks the end of Tasmania's fortnight-long winter arts festival.

image.altText: Crowd of swimmers prepare to enter the water for Dark Mofo's 2026 Nude Solstice Swim

Lionel Messi holds his hands up and smiles

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

children.content: Kylian Mbappe opens the scoring for France against Iraq after Lionel Messi becomes the top scorer in World Cup history. Follow live.

items.title: Wild weather, Mbappe screamers and a Messi milestone at the World Cup
What's been achieved by the US–Iran talks so far

children.content: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

image.altText: JD Vance speaks at a podium.

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

image.altText: A row of men in suits sitting on a stage

items.title: Hantavirus cruise passengers released after 42 days in Perth quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak have completed 42 days of quarantine in Perth and been cleared to return home.

image.altText: A front-on shot of a white van driving at night with headlights on and a police van to the right.

items.title: Australian trust in China grows as belief in US hits record low

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: The hidden dynamic that could undermine global stocks

children.content: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

articles.title: Wall Street mixed as SpaceX and Google drop

children.content: It was a mixed finish on Wall Street after major tech names end in the red. The local share market could rise. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Bird flu biosecurity to face test poultry farmers knew was coming

image.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

image.altText: the earth rotates and is surrounded by tiny reflective particles

items.title: Toll cap cut, new hospitals and schools: Inside NSW budget plans

children.content: NSW Treasurer Daniel Mookhey has tempered expectations of a return to surplus ahead of Tuesday's budget. Here's what has been promised so far.

items.title: This artist 'stole' the Rosetta Stone from the UK and took it home to Egypt

children.content: The Rosetta Stone changed the way we understood Ancient Egypt. It sits in the British Museum but this artist wanted to return it home. To pull it off he needed some legal advice, a digital scanner and the courage to take on the way we look at history.

items.title: Nervous wait for farmers as bird flu investigation expands

children.content: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

articles.title: Second Australian case of deadly bird flu confirmed

children.content: Authorities say a second bird found sick on WA's south coast had the deadly H5 strain of avian flu, as one of the nation's biggest chicken producers announces it is locking down its WA operations.

image.altText: Giant Northern Petrel

articles.title: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next

children.content: Authorities were worried about avian influenza coming in from the north, until the virus started moving across the Southern Ocean. Now infected birds have been found in Western Australia. Where else could birds carrying the virus land?

items.title: Veterans paid extra $7m in abuse compensation after review

children.content: The Defence Force Ombudsman made the extra payments after a federal court case forced it to admit its reparation scheme didn't consider the ongoing impact of assaults.

items.title: MPs to knock off early for war memorial extension opening

children.content: Federal parliament is set to adjourn early today so politicians can attend the official opening of a hall as part of the controversial $550 million extension of the War Memorial.

items.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme

children.content: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

items.title: Tax system favours older Australians over younger, report finds

children.content: Australia's tax policy settings are leading to vastly different outcomes for people who have the same gross incomes but are simply different ages, a new report shows.
